introSection:
Top Thrill 2, formerly known as Top Thrill Dragster, is a steel launched strata roller coaster located at Cedar Point in Sandusky, Ohio, USA. It was designed by Intamin, who subcontracted manufacturing to Stakotra Manufacturing. The ride opened on May 4, 2003 as the first complete circuit roller coaster to exceed in height. It is one of only two strata coasters in existence, the other being Kingda Ka at Six Flags Great Adventure in Jackson, New Jersey. Top Thrill Dragster was the second hydraulically-launched roller coaster built by Intamin, opening a year after Xcelerator at Knott's Berry Farm. The train was originally launched to in 4 seconds.

The ride was installed by Martin & Vleminckx.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
In August 2002, Cedar Point announced that they were building a new roller coaster, and that track was onsite. Two rides, Chaos and Troika, were relocated within the park to make room for the new coaster. Top Thrill Dragster was officially unveiled in January 2003.

On May 4, 2003, the ride opened to the public, being the fastest and the tallest roller coaster in the world. It suffered from technical issues, reopening on July 16 after a period of downtime. The records for tallest and fastest roller coaster were taken two years later by Kingda Ka at Six Flags Great Adventure.

During the 2010 season, the hydraulic systems were overhauled. Top Thrill Dragster was repainted by Baynum Solutions for 2016.



At the start of the 2017 season, the ride was temporarily renamed Top Thrill Cubster after the park lost a bet with Six Flags Great America.

On February 18, 2022, Cedar Point announced that Top Thrill Dragster would not operate at all during the 2022 season. On September 5, 2022, the park released a statement which said Top Thrill Dragster "as you know it is being retired" and that an announcement would be made later about a new and "reimagined" ride experience. Some sections of the track in and around the station were removed and the ride has been repainted gray, with white supports. On January 9, 2023, Cedar Point released a video to their YouTube channel showcasing the ride's top hat element. This video confirmed the ride's reopening date as 2024. On July 18, 2023, Cedar Point made a social media post teasing an announcement on August 1.

Details of the refurbished ride were shared by Zamperla on their website a day before the official announcement, and removed shortly after. On August 1, Cedar Point announced that the ride would reopen as Top Thrill 2 in 2024. Zamperla also confirmed its role in redesigning the ride and constructing new trains. One of the trains was unveiled at the 2023 IAAPA Expo in Orlando, Florida, USA. Construction of the new vertical spike was completed in December 2023.

Top Thrill 2 opened to the media and invited guests on April 25, 2024. It opened to the public on May 4, 2024. On May 12, 2024, the park published a note on social media stating Top Thrill 2 had been closed indefinitely to allow Zamperla to make modifications to the trains. The ride reopened on May 3, 2025.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: Top Thrill 2 uses magnetic brakes provided by InTraSys GmbH.

sections.title: Station
sections.content: Top Thrill 2's station has separate areas for loading and unloading.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: Top Thrill 2 has three trains, with five cars each. Riders are arranged two across in two rows, except for the first car which has only one row. Thus each train seats 18 riders. When Top Thrill Dragster first opened, the final car of each train only had a single row as there was an engine, spoiler, and tires at the back. These theming elements were later removed and seats installed in their place.

sections.title: Rollbacks
sections.content: Occasionally, if the train does not accelerate to the required speed, it will fail to crest the top hat element and fall back onto the launch track again.

sections.title: Incidents
sections.content:
On July 14, 2004, at around 8:20 PM, four people were struck by flying debris while riding the coaster. Reports indicated that its metal cable frayed as the train was being launched. Shards of metal sheared off of it and struck the riders as the train launched. The riders were left with abrasions mainly on their arms and one rider had cuts on his face. The injured ones were treated at the park's first aid station, and two of them later sought further medical attention on their own.

At around 16:30 on August 15, 2021, an L-shaped bracket later determined to be a flag plate fell from a train near the end of the ride and hit a guest standing in the queue line. She was subsequently taken to the hospital, and her condition is not known. Top Thrill Dragster was closed immediately. On August 19, 2021, Cedar Point released a statement stating that Top Thrill Dragster would remain closed for the rest of the season.

sections.title: External links
sections.content: * CedarPoint.com - Top Thrill Dragster's official page

fullText:
Top Thrill 2, formerly known as Top Thrill Dragster, is a steel launched strata roller coaster located at Cedar Point in Sandusky, Ohio, USA. It was designed by Intamin, who subcontracted manufacturing to Stakotra Manufacturing. The ride opened on May 4, 2003 as the first complete circuit roller coaster to exceed in height. It is one of only two strata coasters in existence, the other being Kingda Ka at Six Flags Great Adventure in Jackson, New Jersey. Top Thrill Dragster was the second hydraulically-launched roller coaster built by Intamin, opening a year after Xcelerator at Knott's Berry Farm. The train was originally launched to in 4 seconds.

The ride was installed by Martin & Vleminckx.



In August 2002, Cedar Point announced that they were building a new roller coaster, and that track was onsite. Two rides, Chaos and Troika, were relocated within the park to make room for the new coaster. Top Thrill Dragster was officially unveiled in January 2003.

On May 4, 2003, the ride opened to the public, being the fastest and the tallest roller coaster in the world. It suffered from technical issues, reopening on July 16 after a period of downtime. The records for tallest and fastest roller coaster were taken two years later by Kingda Ka at Six Flags Great Adventure.

During the 2010 season, the hydraulic systems were overhauled. Top Thrill Dragster was repainted by Baynum Solutions for 2016.



At the start of the 2017 season, the ride was temporarily renamed Top Thrill Cubster after the park lost a bet with Six Flags Great America.

On February 18, 2022, Cedar Point announced that Top Thrill Dragster would not operate at all during the 2022 season. On September 5, 2022, the park released a statement which said Top Thrill Dragster "as you know it is being retired" and that an announcement would be made later about a new and "reimagined" ride experience. Some sections of the track in and around the station were removed and the ride has been repainted gray, with white supports. On January 9, 2023, Cedar Point released a video to their YouTube channel showcasing the ride's top hat element. This video confirmed the ride's reopening date as 2024. On July 18, 2023, Cedar Point made a social media post teasing an announcement on August 1.

Details of the refurbished ride were shared by Zamperla on their website a day before the official announcement, and removed shortly after. On August 1, Cedar Point announced that the ride would reopen as Top Thrill 2 in 2024. Zamperla also confirmed its role in redesigning the ride and constructing new trains. One of the trains was unveiled at the 2023 IAAPA Expo in Orlando, Florida, USA. Construction of the new vertical spike was completed in December 2023.

Top Thrill 2 opened to the media and invited guests on April 25, 2024. It opened to the public on May 4, 2024. On May 12, 2024, the park published a note on social media stating Top Thrill 2 had been closed indefinitely to allow Zamperla to make modifications to the trains. The ride reopened on May 3, 2025.

Top Thrill 2 uses magnetic brakes provided by InTraSys GmbH.

Top Thrill 2's station has separate areas for loading and unloading.

Top Thrill 2 has three trains, with five cars each. Riders are arranged two across in two rows, except for the first car which has only one row. Thus each train seats 18 riders. When Top Thrill Dragster first opened, the final car of each train only had a single row as there was an engine, spoiler, and tires at the back. These theming elements were later removed and seats installed in their place.

Occasionally, if the train does not accelerate to the required speed, it will fail to crest the top hat element and fall back onto the launch track again.

On July 14, 2004, at around 8:20 PM, four people were struck by flying debris while riding the coaster. Reports indicated that its metal cable frayed as the train was being launched. Shards of metal sheared off of it and struck the riders as the train launched. The riders were left with abrasions mainly on their arms and one rider had cuts on his face. The injured ones were treated at the park's first aid station, and two of them later sought further medical attention on their own.

At around 16:30 on August 15, 2021, an L-shaped bracket later determined to be a flag plate fell from a train near the end of the ride and hit a guest standing in the queue line. She was subsequently taken to the hospital, and her condition is not known. Top Thrill Dragster was closed immediately. On August 19, 2021, Cedar Point released a statement stating that Top Thrill Dragster would remain closed for the rest of the season.




 * CedarPoint.com - Top Thrill Dragster's official page
